entity.summary: Japan-based centralized cryptocurrency exchange launched in 2014, affected by a major unauthorized outflow disclosed in September 2018, and transferred from Tech Bureau to Fisco Cryptocurrency Exchange in November 2018 before continuing under the Zaif brand.
entity.notes: The 2018 security incident and subsequent business transfer are recorded as continuity events rather than a terminal shutdown. Current official and industry-association sources confirm that the Zaif service remains active under Zaif Inc.

expected.summary: Japan-based centralized exchange launched in 2014 and still operating under the Zaif brand.

changes.summary: Japan-based centralized cryptocurrency exchange launched in 2014, affected by a major unauthorized outflow disclosed in September 2018, and transferred from Tech Bureau to Fisco Cryptocurrency Exchange in November 2018 before continuing under the Zaif brand.
changes.notes: The 2018 security incident and subsequent business transfer are recorded as continuity events rather than a terminal shutdown. Current official and industry-association sources confirm that the Zaif service remains active under Zaif Inc.

events.title: Zaif disclosed a major unauthorized cryptocurrency outflow
events.description: Tech Bureau disclosed that cryptocurrency held for the Zaif service had been unlawfully transferred following external unauthorized access, with later Zaif materials describing losses of roughly 7 billion yen in BTC, MONA, and BCH.
events.notes: September 20, 2018 is used as the public disclosure date. The event did not result in permanent closure of the Zaif service.

events.title: Zaif business transferred from Tech Bureau to Fisco Cryptocurrency Exchange
events.description: The Zaif Exchange and Zaif Instant Exchange businesses were transferred from Tech Bureau to Fisco Cryptocurrency Exchange effective November 22, 2018 as part of the post-incident restructuring and customer-compensation process.
events.notes: The service continued under successor operating entities rather than ending on the transfer date.

evidence.title: Completion of Zaif business succession procedures
evidence.notes: Zaif's official retrospective states that BTC, MONA, and BCH worth about 7 billion yen were lost following unauthorized access in September 2018.

evidence.title: Unauthorized outflow at an association member
evidence.publisher: Japan Virtual and Crypto Assets Exchange Association
evidence.notes: The industry association confirmed Tech Bureau's disclosure of an unauthorized outflow involving customer crypto assets.

evidence.title: Request for customer consent to transfer of liabilities and contract
evidence.notes: The official transfer notice states that the Zaif businesses would move from Tech Bureau to Fisco Cryptocurrency Exchange on November 22, 2018.

evidence.title: Completion of Zaif business succession procedures
evidence.notes: The successor operator's retrospective explains the transfer, customer succession, and compensation process.

evidence.title: Zaif official exchange
evidence.notes: The official domain verifies the current Zaif identity and active exchange service.
